data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project. Let me plan the migration from xUnit.net v2 to v3:

1. **Project file changes**:
   - Remove `xunit` (v2 meta-package) and `xunit.runner.visualstudio`
   - Add `xunit.v3` (the v3 meta-package)
   - Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (not needed for v3 — v3 uses its own runner)
   - Change the project SDK or add the xUnit runner entry point
   - In xUnit v3, test projects are executables, not libraries. So we need `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>`


2. **Namespace and API changes**: The `using Xunit;` directive stays the same, and core attributes like `FactAttribute`, `TheoryAttribute`, and `InlineData` are still available in v3, along with the `Assert` class, though some details may have shifted. I should also check if `global.json` needs updating for the runner configuration.

3. **Project structure for v3**: The package name changes to `xunit.v3`, and test projects need to be configured as executables rather than class libraries. The `xunit.runner.visualstudio` and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` packages aren't required anymore since v3 runs tests directly via `dotnet test` with its own built-in runner.

4. **Custom attributes in v3**: While `FactAttribute` and `TheoryAttribute` remain in the `Xunit` namespace and can still be subclassed, the way custom test attributes work has shifted — I need to verify the exact changes to constructors and interfaces for any custom attributes I'm using. The entry point situation is interesting — xUnit.net v3 uses a source generator to automatically create the entry point, so I don't need to manually add a `Program.cs`. Now I'm looking at this `global.json` file with the `"runner": "VSTest"` configuration. This doesn't match the standard .NET SDK `global.json` format, so it might be a custom xUnit configuration file rather than the typical global.json.

Actually, with xUnit v3, the test runner is built directly into the test assembly itself. When running with `dotnet test`, xUnit v3 uses Microsoft.Testing.Platform instead of VSTest, so I should update that runner configuration accordingly. Since xUnit v3 projects are executables, they can run directly or through `dotnet test` with the proper platform specified.

Let me start mapping out the specific changes needed for the project file. I need to think through how xUnit v3 handles attributes and namespaces. The core attributes like `FactAttribute` and `TheoryAttribute` might be in different namespaces or have API changes compared to earlier versions, so I should probably try building first to see what errors surface, or check the NuGet package details to understand the structure better. The `InlineData` attribute syntax remains the same in v3, so I can proceed with the migration and adjust if any issues come up during the build.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
Contents of https://www.nuget.org/packages/xunit.v3:
*   [README](#readme-body-tab)
*   [Frameworks](#supportedframeworks-body-tab)
*   [Dependencies](#dependencies-body-tab)
*   [Used By](#usedby-body-tab)
*   [Versions](#versions-body-tab)
*   [Release Notes](#releasenotes-body-tab)

## xUnit.net v3

xUnit.net is a developer testing framework, built to support Test Driven Development, with a design goal of extreme simplicity and alignment with framework features. Supports .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later and .NET 8 or later.

Installing this package installs xunit.v3.mtp-v1.

## About xUnit.net

xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for C#, F#, and Visual Basic. xUnit.net v3 supports .NET 8.0 or later, and .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later.

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). It is licensed under [Apache 2](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) (an OSI approved license). The project is [governed](https://xunit.net/governance) by a Project Lead.

For project documentation, please visit the [xUnit.net project home](https://xunit.net/).

*   _New to xUnit.net? Get started with the [.NET SDK](https://xunit.net/docs/getting-started/v3/getting-started)._
*   _Need some help building the source? See [BUILDING.md](https://github.com/xunit/xunit/tree/main/BUILDING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the project? See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/xunit/.github/tree/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the assertion library? See the [suggested contribution workflow](https://github.com/xunit/assert.xunit/tree/main/README.md#suggested-contribution-workflow) in the assertion library project, as it is slightly more complex due to code being spread across two GitHub repositories._

[![Powered by NDepend](https://raw.github.com/xunit/media/main/powered-by-ndepend-transparent.png)](http://www.ndepend.com/)

### Latest Builds

Latest stable

Latest CI ([how to use](https://xunit.net/docs/using-ci-builds))
